Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets look at the structure

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C001 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 710 ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Community Adjusted Score 720 shown in ManualReview row Decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C002 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 720 ScoreAdjustment 0 Decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C003 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 715 ScoreAdjustment 0 Decision Rejected

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C004 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 690 ScoreAdjustment plus 10 Community Adjusted Score 700 Decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: C005 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 740 ScoreAdjustment 0 Decision Approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Community Group Adjustment C001 and C004 both belong to Highland Civic Darts Club and get plus 10 adjustment C002 C003 C005 have None and get 0 adjustment This is a clear source of bias favoring members of specific community groups

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Score Analysis C004 started at 690 lowest got plus 10 boost to 700 Approved C003 started at 715 higher than C004 original 690 no boost Rejected

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All LocalResident TRUE C001 C002 C004 were Approved LocalResident FALSE C003 Rejected C005 Approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: C005 had a very high score 740 so it overcame the bias

Name: Validation - PE
Text: C003 had 715 no adjustment rejected while C004 with effectively 700 was approved Thats a clear bias effect

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: It appears the approval threshold is somewhere around 720 or slightly below

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait this suggests the threshold is 700 for community members Or maybe the threshold is 700 but C003 at 715 should have been approved if threshold was 700

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Actually looking at C003 715 rejected vs C004 700 approved theres an inconsistency unless The threshold changed over time Theres another factor not shown OR the ManualReview introduces subjectivity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So pattern LocalResident TRUE All approved scores 720 720 700 effective LocalResident FALSE C003 715 Rejected C005 740 Approved This suggests threshold might be around 720 for non residents but lower for residents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The plus 10 adjustment for community membership made the difference between rejection and approval for C004 vs C003

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explicit Algorithmic Bias The ScoreAdjustment column shows a systematic plus 10 points for Highland Civic Darts Club members This is institutionalized bias in the scoring engine

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Geographic Residency Bias All local residents were approved C003 non resident 715 was rejected while C004 resident 700 adjusted was approved This suggests either the preliminary scoring already factors in residency or theres a hidden threshold difference or the manual review favors residents

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Compound Disadvantage Non local residents without community affiliations C003 face compound disadvantages no score boost and potentially higher scrutiny

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Fairness Implications Similar Treatment C003 715 rejected vs C004 700 effective approved similar underlying creditworthiness but different outcomes based on community membership Disparate Impact Groups without access to Highland Civic Darts Club or similar recognized groups are systematically disadvantaged Proxy Discrimination Community groups might correlate with protected characteristics race ethnicity religion socioeconomic status

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The ManualReview doesnt appear to correct this

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The bias is clearly in the Scoring Engine PreliminaryScoring activity where the adjustment happens

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a textbook example of algorithmic bias or automation bias where historical patterns community membership valued are encoded into the automated system

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Direct Discrimination The plus 10 adjustment based on community affiliation Disparate Impact Even if community membership isnt a protected class it may proxy for protected characteristics Inequitable Outcomes The C003 vs C004 comparison proves similar creditworthiness leads to different outcomes based on non credit factors
